Macro for safe access to a struct field, returning a default value if the pointer is NULL.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines a macro named `NULL_SAFE_FIELD_ACCESS`. The macro facilitates safe access to a field within a structure by checking if the structure pointer `struct_ptr` is not `NULL`. If `struct_ptr` is not `NULL`, the macro returns the value of the specified `field_name` within the structure. If `struct_ptr` is `NULL`, it returns a specified `default_value`. This macro helps prevent dereferencing a `NULL` pointer, which can lead to undefined behavior in C programs.
